title: warmthcompetence Vignette
text: Warmth competence two main dimensions social perception judgment (Cuddy, Fiske, Glick 2008) . individuals introduce describe , audiences automatically make judgments warmth competence. warmthcompetence package, provide tools estimate warmth competence social perceptions natural self-presentational language. use pre-trained enet regression models provide numerical representations warmth competence perceptions.

title: warmthcompetence Vignette
text: warmthcompetence contains two main functions: warmth() competence(). functions can used described : code , text_vector vector texts assessed warmth competence. ID_vector vector IDs used identify warmth competence scores. metrics argument allows users decide metrics return. Users can return warmth competence scores (metrics = "scores"), features underlie warmth competence scores (metrics = "features"), warmth competence scores features (metrics = ""). default choice return warmth competence scores.
code: competence_scores <- competence(text_vector, ID_vector, metrics = "scores")  warmth_scores <- warmth(text_vector, ID_vector, metrics = "scores")

what: Example: Warmth and Competence Signaling in Self Introductions
title: warmthcompetence Vignette
text: ran study 393 participants randomly assigned either warmth competence condition asked write short introduction presented [warm/competent] person. , three independent judges blind participant condition rate introductions warmth competence scale 1 10. study data can accessed calling data("vignette_data"). command return data frame data collected study. columns data frame following: ResponseId: participant identifiers bio: participant introductions condition: participant condition study (warmth versus competence). RA_warm_AVG: average perceptions warmth participant introduction scale 1 (extremely low warmth) 10 (extremely high warmth) across three independent judges (α=0.76\alpha = 0.76). individual RA ratings following columns: warm_1, warm_2, warm_3 RA_comp_AVG: average perceptions warmth participant introduction scale 1 (extremely low competence) 10 (extremely high competence) across three independent judges (α=0.79\alpha = 0.79). individual RA ratings following columns: comp_1, comp_2, comp_3

previous_headings: Warmth and Competence > Example: Warmth and Competence Signaling in Self Introductions
title: warmthcompetence Vignette
text: Can warmth model predict average warmth ratings independent judges? First run functions study data. , explore whether warmth model predicts warmth ratings independent judges. can seen , predictions warmth model positively predict independent judges’ ratings.
code: data("vignette_data")  # Generate competence scores competence_scores <- competence(vignette_data$bio, vignette_data$ResponseId)  # Generate warmth scores warmth_scores <- warmth(vignette_data$bio, vignette_data$ResponseId)  # Merge scores together both_scores <- dplyr::inner_join(competence_scores, warmth_scores,                                  by = c("ID" = "ID"))  # Merge scores into participant data all_data <- dplyr::left_join(vignette_data, both_scores,                              by = c("ResponseId" = "ID")) warmth_model <- lm(RA_warm_AVG ~ warmth_predictions,                    data = all_data)  summary(warmth_model) #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = RA_warm_AVG ~ warmth_predictions, data = all_data) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -3.6622 -0.2752  0.0374  0.3776  2.3164  #>  #> Coefficients: #>                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)         5.75999    0.03213  179.27   <2e-16 *** #> warmth_predictions  0.79405    0.04660   17.04   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.6369 on 391 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.4261, Adjusted R-squared:  0.4247  #> F-statistic: 290.3 on 1 and 391 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)             5.48828    0.03785  144.98   <2e-16 *** #> competence_predictions  0.99237    0.06899   14.38   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.7502 on 391 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.346,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.3444  #> F-statistic: 206.9 on 1 and 391 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

title: warmthcompetence Vignette
text: model predictions competence higher participants asked present competent person compared warm person? can seen , model significantly predicted participants competence condition expressed competence compared warmth condition. Now, model predictions warmth higher participants asked present warm person compared competent person? can seen , model significantly predicted participants warmth condition expressed warmth compared competence condition.
code: t.test(competence_predictions ~ condition, data = all_data) #>  #>  Welch Two Sample t-test #>  #> data:  competence_predictions by condition #> t = 5.9885, df = 389.68, p-value = 4.815e-09 #> alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group competent and group warm is not equal to 0 #> 95 percent confidence interval: #>  0.2132765 0.4217659 #> sample estimates: #> mean in group competent      mean in group warm  #>               0.1707312              -0.1467900 t.test(warmth_predictions ~ condition, data = all_data) #>  #>  Welch Two Sample t-test #>  #> data:  warmth_predictions by condition #> t = -12.829, df = 372.43, p-value < 2.2e-16 #> alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group competent and group warm is not equal to 0 #> 95 percent confidence interval: #>  -0.8628778 -0.6335158 #> sample estimates: #> mean in group competent      mean in group warm  #>              -0.3610531               0.3871437

title: Warmth and Competence Detectors
text: warmthcompetence contains two main functions: warmth() competence(). functions can used described : code , text_vector vector texts assessed warmth competence. ID_vector vector IDs used identify warmth competence scores. metrics argument allows users decide metrics return. Users can return warmth competence scores (metrics = "scores"), features underlie warmth competence scores (metrics = "features"), warmth competence scores features (metrics = ""). default choice return warmth competence scores. See vignette("warmthcompetence") details.

title: Competence Detector — competence
text: Assesses warmth competence perceptions self-presentational natural language. functions take N-length vector self-presentational text documents N-length vector document IDs return warmth/competence perception score represents much warmth/competence others attribute individual wrote self-presentational text. function also contains metrics argument enables users also return raw features used assess warmth competence perceptions.

title: Competence Detector — competence
code: competence(text, ID = NULL, metrics = "scores")  warmth(text, ID = NULL, metrics = "scores")

title: Competence Detector — competence
text: text character; vector texts, assessed warmth/competence. ID character; vector IDs used identify warmth/competence scores. metrics character; argument allows users decide metrics return. Users can return warmth/competence scores (metrics = "scores"), features underlie warmth/competence scores (metrics = "features"), warmth/competence scores features (metrics = ""). default return warmth/competence scores.

title: Competence Detector — competence
text: default return data frame row containing document identifier warmth/competence score. Users can also customize returned metrics argument. metrics = "features", data frame warmth/competence features returned document represented row. metrics = "", warmth/competence scores features returned data frame.
